raw_response:
# Answer

A Force Majeure Event must persist for more than **120 days** before either Party has the right to terminate the agreement. After 120 days of continued Force Majeure Event, either Party may terminate upon 30 days' written notice.

(Reference: Section 14.5)

# Answer

A Force Majeure Event must persist for more than **one hundred twenty (120) days** before either Party has the right to terminate the Agreement. Upon such termination, the terminating Party must provide thirty (30) days' written notice.

(Reference: Section 14.5)

# Answer

A Force Majeure Event must persist for more than **one hundred twenty (120) days** before either Party has the right to terminate the agreement. After that threshold, either Party may terminate upon thirty (30) days' written notice.

(See Section 14.5)
